title: lappula marginata
intro: Lappula marginata, also known as white spike larkspur, is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Lappula marginata include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
